jokes.text: It's gonna be awesome and... Some other stuff
jokes.setup: Charlie starts promoting his musical with enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Trails off into vague, uninspired description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - starts confident then reveals complete lack of planning. Economy and character integration are strong.

jokes.text: Just to write a musical. Does there have to be a reason? I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Gang questions why he wrote a musical
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely innocent response to obvious suspicion
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Charlie's obliviousness to the gang's natural suspicion is perfectly naive.

jokes.text: Who's the mark?
jokes.setup: Charlie claims he has no ulterior motive
jokes.punchline: Gang immediately assumes there's a con
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - shows how the gang can't conceive of doing anything without an angle.

jokes.text: Whose face are we shoving this musical in?
jokes.setup: Continuing the assumption of ulterior motives
jokes.punchline: Reducing art to petty revenge
jokes.explanation: Escalates the gang's cynicism - they can only understand creative expression as a weapon.

jokes.text: You don't shove a musical in someone's face. What are you talking about?
jokes.punchline: Charlie's bewildered response to their hostility
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Charlie's innocence and the gang's cynical worldview.

jokes.text: The Nightman Cometh.
jokes.setup: Building anticipation for the title reveal
jokes.punchline: Deliberately awkward, pseudo-dramatic title
jokes.explanation: Iconic title that perfectly captures Charlie's pretentious yet childish sensibilities. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Or literate. She added words to it.
jokes.setup: Artemis explains she transcribed Charlie's work
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals Charlie's script was largely illegible
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that reveals Charlie's incompetence through understatement.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah! Lead, of course.
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate assumption he deserves the lead role
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's ego and entitlement - of course he'd be the lead.

jokes.text: Two parts? Oh, yeah!
jokes.setup: Learning he plays both the boy and the dayman
jokes.punchline: Mac's excitement about playing multiple roles
jokes.explanation: Continues Mac's ego trip - more roles means more attention.

jokes.text: I'm the troll guy?
jokes.setup: Charlie assigns Frank as the troll
jokes.punchline: Frank's confused reaction to obvious casting
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag - Frank's size and appearance make the casting obvious but he's oblivious.

jokes.text: Can I do it naked?
jokes.punchline: Immediate request to perform nude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - takes any opportunity to be inappropriate. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: I'm a princess who lives in a coffee shop?
jokes.punchline: Bizarre character concept that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Absurd character concept that reveals Charlie's poor writing and bizarre imagination.

jokes.text: Well, why am I in love with a little boy?
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable realization about the inappropriate plot
jokes.explanation: Creates genuine discomfort while setting up the running gag about Charlie's inappropriate lyrics.

jokes.text: You keep using that word, but I'm not convinced you know what it means.
jokes.punchline: Direct reference to Princess Bride quote
jokes.explanation: Familiar pop culture reference but fits well with Charlie's misuse of the word 'metaphor'.

jokes.text: The nightman's bad-ass, dude. He has the eyes of a cat and does karate across the stage.
jokes.setup: Dennis wanting to play the nightman
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up character attributes
jokes.explanation: Dennis inventing ridiculous character traits shows his need to make everything cooler and more dramatic.

jokes.text: Tiny boy, little boy, Baby boy, I need you
jokes.setup: Dee starting to sing Charlie's song
jokes.punchline: Extremely uncomfortable lyrics sung reluctantly
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - the lyrics are genuinely disturbing but delivered in a musical theater context.

jokes.text: Tiny boy, little boy, Want to make love to you, boy
jokes.punchline: Even more inappropriate lyrics
jokes.explanation: Escalates the discomfort - each line gets worse, creating sustained cringe.

jokes.text: What if it's just a sexually charged embrace?
jokes.punchline: Artemis suggesting an inappropriate alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artemis moment - making things weirder with her theatrical background.

jokes.text: Will you take a five, Dennis?! Take a five, please!
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to get Dennis to leave
jokes.punchline: Increasingly frantic repetition of theater jargon
jokes.explanation: Charlie's pretentious use of theater terminology while having a meltdown.

jokes.text: And what's on the very back page? Nothing. Then that's what you'll be singing.
jokes.setup: Dee asking what to sing instead
jokes.punchline: Charlie cutting her song entirely out of spite
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny moment showing Charlie's petty vindictiveness disguised as artistic control.

jokes.text: Let me try and rember something. Uh, let's see... was it... did Dee write a musical and come to Charlie with it? No, Charlie wrote a musical and came to Dee with it.
jokes.setup: Dee complaining about losing her song
jokes.punchline: Charlie's condescending rhetorical lecture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie meltdown - passive-aggressive and pretentious while making a simple point.

jokes.text: And the gang. And the gang likes to screw it up and make it about themselves and take it away from Charlie and ruin his hopes and dreams.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining the situation to Dee
jokes.punchline: Charlie accurately describing the show's entire premise in third person
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary that works both as character moment and show self-awareness.

jokes.text: Or I could strap on a wig and I could do the song myself.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous threat to perform in drag
jokes.explanation: Escalates Charlie's tantrum to absurd heights - he'd rather perform in drag than compromise.

jokes.text: I'm eating because I'm very uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking what Dennis is doing
jokes.punchline: Dennis's matter-of-fact explanation for stress eating
jokes.explanation: Great economy and character moment - Dennis's honesty about his discomfort is refreshing.

jokes.text: Got to play the troll toll To get into this boy's hole
jokes.punchline: Unfortunate pronunciation making it sound sexual
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment - the pronunciation issue creates genuine discomfort and becomes highly quotable. Near-perfect execution.

jokes.text: I feel like you're saying 'boy's hole', and it's clearly 'soul'.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's oblivious correction
jokes.explanation: Charlie's innocence about the sexual implications makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You got to pay the troll toll to get in this boy's hole... No, see, right there. It sounds like 'hole'.
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately repeating the same pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Escalates the running gag - Frank can't or won't change his pronunciation.

jokes.text: What in God's name are you talking? There's no rape scene.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's horrified denial
jokes.explanation: Charlie's innocent shock at how his musical is being interpreted.

jokes.text: Well, sure, I pay the troll toll, and then I rape Dennis.
jokes.setup: Charlie denying there's a rape scene
jokes.punchline: Mac's matter-of-fact interpretation
jokes.explanation: Mac's casual interpretation of the plot as rape is disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: What if I were to position him in a way where I get behind him, and then Frank could throw that blanket that he's got right there over us, and then that way you can't see the penetration.
jokes.punchline: Mac explicitly describing a rape scene
jokes.explanation: Mac's detailed planning of what he thinks is a rape scene creates maximum discomfort.

jokes.text: Because the blanket will make the rapings... I'm sorry, the sexing from behind feel more classy.
jokes.punchline: Frank's attempt to be tactful while making it worse
jokes.explanation: Frank's 'correction' somehow makes the description even more inappropriate.

jokes.text: Now I'm here, okay? I am, I'm past where I thought I could go. I'm, like, all the way up here with it.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's breakdown using vague stress metaphors
jokes.explanation: Charlie's stress level metaphors perfectly capture his complete overwhelm.

jokes.text: Oh, wow, a free ticket to a play that I don't want to see.
jokes.punchline: Waitress's completely uninterested response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response highlighting how unwanted Charlie's attention is.

jokes.text: you're not gonna call me at 3:00 in the morning and tell me that you are in the emergency room in the hospital because you had a terrible car accident?
jokes.setup: Charlie promising to leave her alone
jokes.punchline: Specific examples of his stalking behavior
jokes.explanation: Reveals the extent of Charlie's inappropriate behavior through specific examples.

jokes.text: Well, yeah, because I consider you that.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's sincere but inappropriate declaration
jokes.explanation: Charlie's sincere belief in their relationship creates genuine discomfort.

jokes.text: I forgot to tell you, Calvin Coolidge was a good friend of mine.
jokes.setup: Gladys being introduced as the pianist
jokes.punchline: Impossible claim about a president who died decades ago
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist moment showing Gladys is clearly very elderly and confused.

jokes.text: I can't really move my arms in this thing, so I think I need to rip the pits.
jokes.punchline: Practical costume problems ruining the dramatic effect
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Dennis's vanity being undermined by practical concerns.

jokes.text: I wrote a song. I'm gonna throw it in.
jokes.punchline: Mac casually announcing he's adding unauthorized content
jokes.explanation: Mac's selfishness and poor timing perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I will smack your face off of your face.
jokes.setup: Mac wanting to add a song
jokes.punchline: Charlie's redundant threat
jokes.explanation: Charlie's stress creating nonsensical threats with redundant phrasing.

jokes.text: Just to be clear, I did not write that song,
jokes.punchline: Dee immediately distancing herself from the content
jokes.explanation: Dee breaking character to clarify she's not responsible for the inappropriate content.

jokes.text: Most men find me to be an eight or nine out of ten. And I am available to any interested men. Who'd like to get my number after the show.
jokes.setup: Dee still on stage after her disclaimer
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate personal ad during the performance
jokes.explanation: Dee's desperation and social obliviousness creating maximum cringe.

jokes.text: Keep singing, bitch! You're not gonna have a face by the time I'm done with you!
jokes.setup: Dee going off-script with her personal ad
jokes.punchline: Charlie's violent threats from backstage
jokes.explanation: Charlie's complete loss of composure as his artistic vision crumbles.

jokes.text: I was hearing a lot of laughs out there, bro. Laughs are cheap. I'm going for gasps.
jokes.punchline: Dennis's pretentious dismissal of audience enjoyment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - dismissing actual entertainment for pretentious shock value. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Confound your lousy toll, troll.
jokes.punchline: Mac's overly dramatic, old-fashioned curse
jokes.explanation: Mac's attempt at Shakespearean drama comes off as ridiculous.

jokes.text: Dude, do you have a boner right now?
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis in the scene together
jokes.punchline: Dennis noticing Mac's inappropriate physical reaction
jokes.explanation: Maximum cringe moment that breaks the fourth wall of the performance.

jokes.text: Shut up, dude, don't ruin this for me.
jokes.punchline: Mac's complete lack of shame
jokes.explanation: Mac's selfish plea to continue his inappropriate moment.

jokes.text: Come over here and scratch my itchy-witchy toesy-woesies.
jokes.setup: Dennis as the nightman trying to be menacing
jokes.punchline: Baby talk ruining any sense of threat
jokes.explanation: Dennis's attempt at menacing villain voice becomes ridiculous baby talk.

jokes.text: What the hell is that? / You know what it is, bitch.
jokes.punchline: Mac's aggressive confidence as the Dayman
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff with Mac's sudden badass persona switch.

jokes.text: You're skipping a line, dude. You're still skipping the same line. Just move past it.
jokes.punchline: Dennis breaking character to give direction
jokes.explanation: Meta moment showing the performance falling apart in real time.

jokes.text: What? No, don't improv. Don't tell me what to do.
jokes.punchline: Mac's rebellious response leading to more chaos
jokes.explanation: Mac's ego preventing him from taking direction, making the performance worse.

jokes.text: Body bag.
jokes.punchline: Mac's attempt at a cool finishing line
jokes.explanation: Mac's attempt to add a cool action movie line to the musical.

jokes.text: Dayman, Fighter of the Nightman, Champion of the sun, You're a master of karate and friendship for everyone.
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical combination of 'master of karate and friendship'
jokes.explanation: Iconic song that became a cultural phenomenon. The absurd pairing of violence and friendship is perfect.

jokes.text: Don't say 'stage freeze.' Just do it.
jokes.punchline: Charlie correcting theater technique from offstage
jokes.explanation: Charlie's directorial control issues extending to basic stage directions.

jokes.text: Please say yes and do not bone me, please.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally sexual plea at the end
jokes.explanation: Charlie's innocent attempt at rhyming creates an inappropriate double entendre.

jokes.text: Oh, I am definitely, definitely saying no.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking if she's saying no to marriage
jokes.punchline: Emphatic rejection with redundant emphasis
jokes.explanation: The redundant 'definitely, definitely' emphasizes how clearly rejected Charlie is.

jokes.text: Well, I didn't ever sign anything, so...
jokes.setup: Waitress saying she never has to see him again
jokes.punchline: Charlie finding a loophole in their verbal agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - finding technical loopholes to continue stalking behavior.

jokes.text: Told you nobody just writes a musical for no reason.
jokes.setup: The musical clearly being about Charlie's obsession
jokes.punchline: Mac calling back to his earlier suspicion
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Mac was right to be suspicious from the beginning.

jokes.text: I thought the rape scene went really well.
jokes.punchline: Frank's positive review of what wasn't supposed to be rape
jokes.explanation: Frank's oblivious satisfaction with what he perceived as a rape scene.
